description_blurb: This report summarizes the historical record of launches occurring at Vandenberg Air Force Base between 1958 and 2000.
title: DOW-UAP-D49, Launch Summary, Vandenberg AFB, 2000
summary: This report summarizes the historical record of all major launch operations conducted from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California, between 1958 and 2000. It serves as an official registry of launches presented in chronological order.
bust_reasoning: This document is a 'Launch Summary' from Vandenberg AFB's Office of History, detailing known launch operations from 1958-2000. It is a purely administrative and historical record, containing no narrative of any observed phenomenon, no witness accounts, no sensor data, and no description of any unidentified object or its behavior. As such, there is no UAP incident or observation within the document to assess for mundane explanations.
full_text:
# DOW-UAP-D49, Launch Summary, Vandenberg AFB, 2000 - Page 1

VANDENBERG AFB
LAUNCH SUMMARY
1958 - 2000

[Image: 30th Space Wing emblem containing the text "30TH SPACE WING"]

30th SPACE WING
Office of History

As of: 3 February 2000

i



# DOW-UAP-D49, Launch Summary, Vandenberg AFB, 2000 - Page 3

# FOREWORD

This is the official registry of all major launch operations conducted from Vandenberg Air Force Base, California. The vehicles are presented in chronological order beginning with the first launch on 16 December 1958. Launch dates reflect Vandenberg local time.

The information is further grouped into two matrixes titled: "ANNUAL LAUNCH SUMMARY BY BOOSTER" and "ANNUAL LAUNCH SUMMARY BY COMMAND." Two additional items contained in this document are a "LAUNCH FACILITY GUIDE" and a "GLOSSARY."

In the main chronology section, the two columns reporting cumulative numbers by booster and command represent only Vandenberg operations. They do not include, for instance, Titan IV launches conducted on the East Coast at Cape Canaveral, Florida.

The 30th Space Wing History Office (30th SW/HO) periodically updates the Vandenberg AFB Launch Summary book. Questions may be directed to the undersigned at DSN 275-6421 or commercially at (805) 605-6421.

JEFFREY GEIGER
Office of History

^1 The Pegasus flight program began with three "captive" flights using inert vehicles. These missions were conducted on 9 Nov 89, 15 Dec 89, and 30 Jan 90. The first seven "live" Pegasus flights, beginning on 5 April 90, as well as the inert flights, originated from the Ames/Dryden Flight Research facility at Edwards AFB, California. They were carried aloft by a modified B-52 aircraft provided by NASA. Beginning with the eight mission, Pegasus was carried by a modified Lockheed L-1011 aircraft and was staged from Vandenberg AFB.
^2 The Delta II program was operated by McDonnell Douglas Aerospace until the company was purchased by Boeing Aerospace, 4 August 1997.

title_zh: DOW-UAP-D49, 发射概要, 范登堡空军基地, 2000年
summary_zh: 本报告概述了1958年至2000年期间在加利福尼亚州范登堡空军基地进行的所有主要发射行动的历史记录。本报告作为按时间顺序排列的官方发射登记册。
bust_reasoning_zh: 本文件是范登堡空军基地历史办公室的“发射概要”，详细记录了1958年至2000年期间已知的发射行动。这是一份纯粹的行政和历史记录，不包含任何观测现象的叙述、无目击者证词、无传感器数据，亦无任何不明物体及其行为的描述。因此，本文件内不存在可供评估世俗解释的UAP事件或观测记录。
cover_up_reasoning_zh: 本文件是已知发射活动的历史概要，而非事件报告。文件中未描述任何可能被隐瞒的观测叙述或具体事件。本文件似乎是第30航天联队历史办公室一份标准、公开出版的历史记录，未见任何与UAP事件相关的删节或信息压制迹象。
